No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to health, well-being, or related medical statistics.

Quality Education.reason: The course is an 'Advanced Mathematical Statistics' program at the master's level, directly providing quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and relevant skills for employment (Target 4.4) through statistical abilities.

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality or related targets.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to water, sanitation, or related environmental issues.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course equips students with 'statistical computing and data analysis abilities' and problem-solving skills, contributing to productive employment (Target 8.5) and technological upgrading (Target 8.2).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly supports Target 9.5 by teaching 'theoretical derivation for advanced statistical methods' and how to 'plan and design experiments and surveys' to enhance scientific research and innovation.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to reducing income, social, or other forms of inequality.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to urban development, housing, or sustainable infrastructure for communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable consumption, waste reduction, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or related policies.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or sustainable land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The 'knowledge of professional ethics' competency (B) provides a foundational, albeit indirect, link to fostering accountable institutions (Target 16.6) and reducing corruption (Target 16.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's focus on 'statistical computing and data analysis abilities' and 'standard statistical procedures' directly supports statistical capacity-building (Target 17.19) and producing high-quality data (Target 17.18).
